mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions triggers recession fears that compress equity risk premiums, causing the S&P 500 to decline over the subsequent 5 trading days as investors price in lower near-term cash flows.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in consumer credit expansion and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window, or individual series ICs explain > 80% of the joint signal's predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which lowers input cost inflation and supports corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding the historical median directly increases expected corporate earnings per share, leading to immediate equity repricing and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: The signal's directional accuracy falls below 55% over a 5-year backtest, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between above-median and below-median GDP growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Prolonged high policy rates combined with persistent inflation compress real disposable income and elevate borrowing costs, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in this regime are not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth deceleration and unemployment rate acceleration signal a labor market inflection point, triggering algorithmic risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline by at least 5% in directional accuracy over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive variance beyond the sum of individual series.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer credit growth coinciding with positive GDP surprises indicates resilient household balance sheets, which supports near-term retail and service sector earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day window.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when both series rise concurrently compared to periods of credit contraction, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
